bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for myself. The opponent’s opening offer is valid but only gives me moderate utility, and with no pattern of concessions yet I should anchor on my strongest values across the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (~0.961) because it is still the very start of the negotiation (t=0.02), so there is little need to concede on important issues yet. The opponent’s second offer is worse for me than their first, so they have not shown meaningful concession; I will only concede on the lowest-weight issueD, where they previously offered valueC.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.93 rather than making major concessions. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and show no clear concession trend, so I will hold firm on the three high-weight issues while varying the low-weight issueD to avoid repeating my last exact bid.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me while avoiding an exact repeat of my last offer. The opponent’s offers give me low utility and show no clear concession trend, but they repeatedly use issueD=valueC and avoid my preferred issueC=valueB, so I concede moderately on issueC and keep my highest-weight issueA/issueB at top values.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers have not improved for me; their latest bid is only about 0.33 utility to me. I am conceding only on the lowest-weight issueD, using a value they have previously offered, while holding firm on my three high-importance issues.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a visible concession on my lower-weight issueD and a moderate concession on issueC. The opponent’s offers consistently favor issueC:valueC and issueD:valueC/valueD, but their offers are only around 0.33–0.45 utility to me, so I will hold firm on the high-weight issueA and issueB for now.

I am targeting a high but not maximal utility (~0.877) because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to concede deeply yet. The opponent’s latest offer improved my utility by giving me issueB:valueA, but they continue to push issueC:valueC and issueD:valueC, so I am keeping my strongest positions on issueA/issueB while making a moderate concession on issueC and accepting their preferred-looking issueD:valueC.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a low-cost concession on issueD. The opponent’s recent offers have improved for me, especially by conceding issueA to valueA, but they still push issueC/valueC and issueD/valueC; I will hold firm on the high-weight issues and concede issueD since it matters least to me.

I am targeting a high but slightly more conciliatory utility (about 0.877), since it is still early at t=0.16 and there is no need to drop close to the opponent’s current bids yet. The opponent’s recent offers have improved for me mainly on issueA/issueB, while they repeatedly choose issueC: valueC and issueD: valueC; I will hold firm on the two highest-value issueA/issueB positions and make a modest concession on issueC plus the low-weight issueD.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession on the low-weight issueD. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat on issueA and issueB at times, but they keep pushing issueC:valueC, so I will hold firm on my most valuable issueC:valueB for now rather than concede too much too early.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid rather than making large concessions; this bid preserves my strongest positions on issueA and issueB while conceding some value on issueC and issueD. The opponent’s offers remain low for my utility and mostly emphasize issueC/valueC and issueD/valueC, so I am offering issueD/valueC but not yet dropping all the way on issueC.

At t=0.22 we are still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for me while making only a low-cost concession on issueD, which the opponent frequently sets to valueC. The opponent’s offers are valid but not steadily improving for me—their last offer is worth about 0.487 to me and is below their best prior offer—so I should not concede much on the high-weight issues yet. This bid keeps my strongest values on issueA, issueB, and issueC, while avoiding repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.81 by holding firm on my most important top choices for issueA and issueB while conceding issueC and issueD toward values the opponent has repeatedly proposed. At t=0.24 it is still early enough not to make deep concessions, but the opponent consistently offers issueC:valueC and issueD:valueC, so this bid tests a tradeoff without dropping too low.

I am targeting a utility around 0.79, conceding moderately because we are still early-mid negotiation at t=0.26 and should not drop to the opponent’s current 0.637-value offer yet. The opponent’s bids fluctuate but repeatedly emphasize issueC=valueC and issueD=valueC, while their latest offer gives me issueA=valueA and issueB=valueC, so I concede issueB and issueD while holding firm on high-value issueC=valueB.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.81, since it is only t=0.28 and there is no need to concede deeply yet. The opponent consistently offers issueC:valueC and issueD:valueC, so I am conceding those while holding firm on the high-weight issueA and issueB values that matter most to me.

At t=0.30 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.79, conceding moderately but not dropping to the opponent’s latest low-utility offer. The opponent has repeatedly signaled interest in issueD=valueC and has varied issueB more than issueC, so I concede issueD and offer issueB=valueB while holding my strongest positions on issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid (about 0.787), keeping my high-value positions on issueA and issueC while conceding on issueD and offering issueB as valueC, which is equally acceptable to me as valueB. At t=0.32 there is still time, so I should not drop to the opponent’s latest bid yet, though their history suggests they often prefer issueC:valueC and issueD:valueC while varying issueA/issueB.

At t=0.34 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.81 while making a meaningful concession on issueC and issueD, which the opponent consistently offers as valueC. The opponent has not clearly conceded toward my top issueC=valueB, but they have sometimes offered issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA, so I will hold firm on the two 0.3-weight issues where I can still get maximum utility.

I am targeting a utility around 0.79: still strong for me, but with meaningful concessions now that we are in the mid-game. The opponent has repeatedly pushed issueC: valueC and issueD: valueC, while their offers to me are not clearly improving and the latest is relatively low, so I will concede on issueB and issueD but hold my highest-priority gains on issueA and issueC. There are no opponent messages to factor in.

I am targeting a strong mid-game utility around 0.81 by holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B while conceding issueC to valueC and issueD to valueC, which the opponent has repeatedly signaled in their offers. At t=0.38 there is still time, and the opponent is not steadily conceding, so I should not drop all the way to their best prior bid yet, but this offer is more compatible with their pattern than my maximum bid.

I targeted a strong mid-game utility around 0.79, conceding on lower-cost issueD and moderately on issueB while holding issueA and issueC where my utility remains high. The opponent has repeatedly offered issueD:valueC and often issueB:valueB, but they have not shown consistent concession and their latest offer is low for me, so I am not yet dropping to their best prior bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.79, which is a meaningful mid-game concession while still protecting my high-weight issues. The opponent has repeatedly pushed issueC and issueD toward valueC, so I concede issueD to valueC but hold issueC at valueB because it is highly valuable to me; issueB valueC is low-cost for me and appears acceptable in their history.

I am targeting about 0.81 utility: conceding issueA to valueB and issueD to valueC while holding my strongest positions on issueB and issueC. At t=0.44 it is mid-negotiation, so some concession is appropriate, and the opponent’s latest valid offer signals willingness on issueA=valueB and issueB=valueA while they keep pushing issueC=valueC. Since issueC is highly important to me, I will keep issueC=valueB rather than match their lower valueC yet.

At t=0.46 I am still targeting a strong utility around 0.81, conceding on issueC and issueD while holding my highest-weight issueA and issueB at my best values. The opponent’s offers consistently include issueC: valueC and issueD: valueC, so I am accommodating those signals while not over-conceding before the late stage.

At t=0.48 I am targeting a solid mid-game utility while improving acceptability by using values the opponent has repeatedly signaled: issueC=valueC and issueD=valueC. The opponent has held fairly firm on issueC=valueC, so I concede there, but I keep issueA and issueB at my strongest values rather than dropping to their latest low-utility bid.

At t=0.50 I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.79 rather than dropping to the opponent’s low-utility bids. The opponent repeatedly signals interest in issueB=valueB and issueD=valueC, so I concede those while holding stronger positions on issueA and issueC, and there are no opponent messages to alter that read.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.81: issueA and issueB remain at my top values, while I concede on issueC and the low-weight issueD. At t=0.52, moderate concession is appropriate, and the opponent has repeatedly signaled interest in issueC=valueC and issueD=valueC while their latest offer already included issueB=valueA.

At t=0.54, I am targeting a mid-game compromise around utility 0.637 for me rather than conceding all the way to the opponent’s last offer utility of about 0.571. The opponent’s bids strongly signal they want issueC=valueC and issueD=valueC, while they have previously offered issueA=valueA with issueB=valueB, so I am conceding on issueC/issueD but holding issueA at my best value.
